1 --- 2 title: "Building Channel Plugins" 3 sidebarTitle: "Channel Plugins" 4 summary: "Step-by-step guide to building a messaging channel plugin for OpenClaw" 5 read_when: 6 - You are building a new messaging channel plugin 7 - You want to connect OpenClaw to a messaging platform 8 - You need to understand the ChannelPlugin adapter surface 9 --- 10 11 # Building Channel Plugins 12 13 This guide walks through building a channel plugin that connects OpenClaw to a 14 messaging platform. By the end you will have a working channel with DM security, 15 pairing, reply threading, and outbound messaging. 16 17 18 If you have not built any OpenClaw plugin before, read 19 [Getting Started](/plugins/building-plugins) first for the basic package 20 structure and manifest setup. 21 22 23 ## How channel plugins work 24 25 Channel plugins do not need their own send/edit/react tools. OpenClaw keeps one 26 shared `message` tool in core. Your plugin owns: 27 28 - **Config** — account resolution and setup wizard 29 - **Security** — DM policy and allowlists 30 - **Pairing** — DM approval flow 31 - **Session grammar** — how provider-specific conversation ids map to base chats, thread ids, and parent fallbacks 32 - **Outbound** — sending text, media, and polls to the platform 33 - **Threading** — how replies are threaded 34 35 Core owns the shared message tool, prompt wiring, the outer session-key shape, 36 generic `:thread:` bookkeeping, and dispatch. 37 38 If your platform stores extra scope inside conversation ids, keep that parsing 39 in the plugin with `messaging.resolveSessionConversation(...)`. That is the 40 canonical hook for mapping `rawId` to the base conversation id, optional thread 41 id, explicit `baseConversationId`, and any `parentConversationCandidates`. 42 When you return `parentConversationCandidates`, keep them ordered from the 43 narrowest parent to the broadest/base conversation. 44 45 Bundled plugins that need the same parsing before the channel registry boots 46 can also expose a top-level `session-key-api.ts` file with a matching 47 `resolveSessionConversation(...)` export. Core uses that bootstrap-safe surface 48 only when the runtime plugin registry is not available yet. 49 50 `messaging.resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` remains available as a 51 legacy compatibility fallback when a plugin only needs parent fallbacks on top 52 of the generic/raw id. If both hooks exist, core uses 53 `resolveSessionConversation(...).parentConversationCandidates` first and only 54 falls back to `resolveParentConversationCandidates(...)` when the canonical hook 55 omits them. 56 57 ## Approvals and channel capabilities 58 59 Most channel plugins do not need approval-specific code. 60 61 - Core owns same-chat `/approve`, shared approval button payloads, and generic fallback delivery. 62 - Prefer one `approvalCapability` object on the channel plugin when the channel needs approval-specific behavior. 63 - `approvalCapability.authorizeActorAction` and `approvalCapability.getActionAvailabilityState` are the canonical approval-auth seam. 64 - Use `outbound.shouldSuppressLocalPayloadPrompt` or `outbound.beforeDeliverPayload` for channel-specific payload lifecycle behavior such as hiding duplicate local approval prompts or sending typing indicators before delivery. 65 - Use `approvalCapability.delivery` only for native approval routing or fallback suppression. 66 - Use `approvalCapability.render` only when a channel truly needs custom approval payloads instead of the shared renderer. 67 - If a channel can infer stable owner-like DM identities from existing config, use `createResolvedApproverActionAuthAdapter` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` to restrict same-chat `/approve` without adding approval-specific core logic. 68 - If a channel needs native approval delivery, keep channel code focused on target normalization and transport hooks. Use `createChannelExecApprovalProfile`, `createChannelNativeOriginTargetResolver`, `createChannelApproverDmTargetResolver`, `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalCapability`, and `createChannelNativeApprovalRuntime` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-runtime` so core owns request filtering, routing, dedupe, expiry, and gateway subscription. 69 - Native approval channels must route both `accountId` and `approvalKind` through those helpers. `accountId` keeps multi-account approval policy scoped to the right bot account, and `approvalKind` keeps exec vs plugin approval behavior available to the channel without hardcoded branches in core. 70 - `createApproverRestrictedNativeApprovalAdapter` still exists as a compatibility wrapper, but new code should prefer the capability builder and expose `approvalCapability` on the plugin. 71 72 Auth-only channels can usually stop at the default path: core handles approvals and the plugin just exposes outbound/auth capabilities. Native approval channels such as Matrix, Slack, Telegram, and custom chat transports should use the shared native helpers instead of rolling their own approval lifecycle. 73 74 ## Walkthrough 75 76 77 78 79 Create the standard plugin files. The `channel` field in `package.json` is 80 what makes this a channel plugin: 81 82 83 ```json package.json 84 { 85 "name": "@myorg/openclaw-acme-chat", 86 "version": "1.0.0", 87 "type": "module", 88 "openclaw": { 89 "extensions": ["./index.ts"], 90 "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts", 91 "channel": { 92 "id": "acme-chat", 93 "label": "Acme Chat", 94 "blurb": "Connect OpenClaw to Acme Chat." 95 } 96 } 97 } 98 ``` 99 100 ```json openclaw.plugin.json 101 { 102 "id": "acme-chat", 103 "kind": "channel", 104 "channels": ["acme-chat"], 105 "name": "Acme Chat", 106 "description": "Acme Chat channel plugin", 107 "configSchema": { 108 "type": "object", 109 "additionalProperties": false, 110 "properties": { 111 "acme-chat": { 112 "type": "object", 113 "properties": { 114 "token": { "type": "string" }, 115 "allowFrom": { 116 "type": "array", 117 "items": { "type": "string" } 118 } 119 } 120 } 121 } 122 } 123 } 124 ``` 125 126 127 128 129 130 The `ChannelPlugin` interface has many optional adapter surfaces. Start with 131 the minimum — `id` and `setup` — and add adapters as you need them. 132 133 Create `src/channel.ts`: 134 135 ```typescript src/channel.ts 136 import { 137 createChatChannelPlugin, 138 createChannelPluginBase, 139 } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 140 import type { OpenClawConfig } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core"; 141 import { acmeChatApi } from "./client.js"; // your platform API client 142 143 type ResolvedAccount = { 144 accountId: string | null; 145 token: string; 146 allowFrom: string[]; 147 dmPolicy: string | undefined; 148 }; 149 150 function resolveAccount( 151 cfg: OpenClawConfig, 152 accountId?: string | null, 153 ): ResolvedAccount { 154 const section = (cfg.channels as Record)?.["acme-chat"]; 155 const token = section?.token; 156 if (!token) throw new Error("acme-chat: token is required"); 157 return { 158 accountId: accountId ?? null, 159 token, 160 allowFrom: section?.allowFrom ?? [], 161 dmPolicy: section?.dmSecurity, 162 }; 163 } 164 165 export const acmeChatPlugin = createChatChannelPlugin({ 166 base: createChannelPluginBase({ 167 id: "acme-chat", 168 setup: { 169 resolveAccount, 170 inspectAccount(cfg, accountId) { 171 const section = 172 (cfg.channels as Record)?.["acme-chat"]; 173 return { 174 enabled: Boolean(section?.token), 175 configured: Boolean(section?.token), 176 tokenStatus: section?.token ? "available" : "missing", 177 }; 178 }, 179 }, 180 }), 181 182 // DM security: who can message the bot 183 security: { 184 dm: { 185 channelKey: "acme-chat", 186 resolvePolicy: (account) => account.dmPolicy, 187 resolveAllowFrom: (account) => account.allowFrom, 188 defaultPolicy: "allowlist", 189 }, 190 }, 191 192 // Pairing: approval flow for new DM contacts 193 pairing: { 194 text: { 195 idLabel: "Acme Chat username", 196 message: "Send this code to verify your identity:", 197 notify: async ({ target, code }) => { 198 await acmeChatApi.sendDm(target, `Pairing code: ${code}`); 199 }, 200 }, Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. // Private runtime barrel for the bundled IRC extension.
// Keep this barrel thin and aligned with the local extension surface.

export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  createChannelPairingController,
  DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID,
  deliverFormattedTextWithAttachments,
  dispatchInboundReplyWithBase,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  GROUP_POLICY_BLOCKED_LABEL,
  isDangerousNameMatchingEnabled,
  logInboundDrop,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
  readStoreAllowFromForDmPolicy,
  resolveAllowlistProviderRuntimeGroupPolicy,
  resolveControlCommandGate,
  resolveDefaultGroupPolicy,
  resolveEffectiveAllowFromLists,
  warnMissingProviderGroupPolicyFallbackOnce,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  BlockStreamingCoalesceConfig,
  ChannelPlugin,
  DmConfig,
  DmPolicy,
  GroupPolicy,
  GroupToolPolicyBySenderConfig,
  GroupToolPolicyConfig,
  MarkdownConfig,
  OpenClawConfig,
  OutboundReplyPayload,
  PluginRuntime,
  RuntimeEnv,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc";

content.content:
1	import {
2	  buildChannelConfigSchema,
3	  BlockStreamingCoalesceSchema,
4	  DmConfigSchema,
5	  DmPolicySchema,
6	  GroupPolicySchema,
7	  MarkdownConfigSchema,
8	  ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
9	  ToolPolicySchema,
10	  requireOpenAllowFrom,
11	} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema";
12	import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
13	import { ircChannelConfigUiHints } from "./config-ui-hints.js";
14	
15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
86	}).superRefine((value, ctx) => {
87	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
88	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
89	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});
99

## Created: `extensions/pulseboard/` — 19 files

### Root package files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `package.json` | npm manifest with `openclaw.channel` metadata (id `pulseboard`, text chunk limit note, no runtime deps needed) |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest declaring `id: "pulseboard"` and `channels: ["pulseboard"]` |
| `index.ts` | Entry point — calls `defineChannelPluginEntry` with the plugin and runtime setter |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup phase entry point — calls `defineSetupPluginEntry` |
| `api.ts` | Public barrel re-exporting accounts and setup-surface helpers |

### `src/` implementation
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `runtime-api.ts` | Private barrel importing from individual public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` paths (uses `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` only for `chunkTextForOutbound` and `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase`, which have no standalone generic path) |
| `types.ts` | `PulseboardAccountConfig`, `PulseboardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseboardInboundMessage`, `PulseboardProbe` |
| `config-schema.ts` | Zod schemas for account/config; `buildChannelConfigSchema` wrapper; validates `dmPolicy="open"` requires `allowFrom: ["*"]` |
| `accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseboardAccount`, `listPulseboardAccountIds`; env vars `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN` / `PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL` for the default account |
| `normalize.ts` | `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget` (`#board` / `username`), `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`, `resolvePulseboardAllowlistMatch` (username-based, supports `*` wildcard) |
| `policy.ts` | `resolvePulseboardBoardMatch`, `resolvePulseboardBoardAccessGate`, `resolvePulseboardRequireMention`, `resolvePulseboardBoardSenderAllowed` |
| `runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` wrapper — `getPulseboardRuntime` / `setPulseboardRuntime` |
| `send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseboard` — POSTs to `/api/v1/messages` with Bearer auth |
| `probe.ts` | `probePulseboard` — GETs `/api/v1/ping` with timeout, returns `PulseboardProbe` |
| `monitor.ts` | `monitorPulseboardProvider` — long-polls `/api/v1/messages/poll?since=<id>`, dispatches inbound via `handlePulseboardInbound` |
| `inbound.ts` | Full inbound pipeline: DM policy (pairing/allowlist/open/disabled), board group policy, mention gating, control command gating, `dispatchInboundReplyWithBase` |
| `setup-core.ts` | `pulseboardSetupAdapter`, `updatePulseboardAccountConfig`, `setPulseboardDmPolicy`, `setPulseboardGroupAccess` |
| `setup-surface.ts` | `pulseboardSetupWizard` — interactive wizard for API token, server URL, board access, DM allowlist, mention preference |
| `channel.ts` | `pulseboardPlugin` assembled with `createChatChannelPlugin` — direct + group chat types, 4000-char chunk limit, pairing, security warnings, full directory/status/gateway adapters |
